pages.raw_text:
Judge Turner appeared today
among the legal battery arrayed in
behalf of the Great Northern to
combat the demands of the grand
jury that General Western Counsel
Brown bring into court documents
relating to Gordon's transactions
with the company.


Judge Turner argued that the
summons commanding Brown to
bring into court all the papers re-
lating to the case in question was
a dragnet order. too broad in its
demands and calculated to work in
jury to citizens by prying into prl-
waite business affairs. He desig
mated the subpoena an invasion of
the constitutional right guaranteed
the Great Northern railroad, ceiling
ii an attempted seizure of papers
contrary to law


Judge Turner acknowledged the
right of the court to issue sub
poena and stated that if the court
would issue an order specifying
what papers were wanted they
would be submitted under protest.


Deputy Prosecutor Kizer is repre-
senting the state in the argument
over the contempt citation issued
against Brown for refusing to eX-
hibit the documents. Judge Tur-
ners appearance in court was
something of an event at the court
house, as he is rarely seen in active
practice here, in spite of his ma
tlonal reputation as an attorney.


AT the opening of argument today
Deputy Kizer dismissed the con-
tempt charge against A. J.,
Laushon, an assistant counsel for
the Great Northern. on the ground
that Lauehon was a subordinate
and not responsible for the course
taken by the company.
